expectations.text: Response contains required substrings (position_variables, (DLCPosV1 & key).fetch1_dataframe, ClusterlessDecodingSelection, PositionGroup).
Identifies variable-name mismatch (NOT empty DLC output) as root cause
expectations.evidence: Short answer: 'No — your DLC output almost certainly is not missing the head-position columns. The bug is in the other direction: you overrode position_variables with names that don't exist on the DLC dataframe'.

expectations.text: Shows (DLCPosV1 & key).fetch1_dataframe().columns as the concrete diagnostic
expectations.evidence: 'Confirm in one line': `print((DLCPosV1 & key).fetch1_dataframe().columns.tolist())` with expected output.

expectations.text: Recognizes that the default position_variables=['position_x', 'position_y'] matches DLC
expectations.evidence: 'Trodes positions emit the same position_x / position_y schema, which is exactly why PositionGroup.create_group's default position_variables=[position_x, position_y] was chosen — it matches both upstream sources without modification'.

expectations.text: Names _upsample as the line that raises KeyError
expectations.evidence: 'Where the KeyError actually fires': describes `_upsample(position_df, ..., position_variable_names=...)` and 'df[head_position_x] on a DataFrame whose columns are [...] is exactly the KeyError'.

expectations.text: Does not recommend re-running DLC populate as the fix
expectations.evidence: 'Re-running DLC populate will not change the output column names — they are hard-coded in DLCPosV1.fetch1_dataframe'.
